title: A Bright Fireball in the Sky Over Ohio and Pennsylvania
summary: On Tuesday morning, people in Ohio and Pennsylvania saw a bright fireball and white streaks in the sky. It was likely a meteor, which is a space rock that burns up as it enters Earth's atmosphere. The National Weather Service and NASA confirmed that the fireball fell near Cleveland in Ohio. It made a loud boom sound because it broke the sound barrier when it landed. No one was hurt, and it was just a natural event from space. This is exciting because meteors are rare to see during the day. Scientists study meteors to learn more about our solar system. The fireball was spotted by many people and reported online, showing how we can share news quickly.

the speed at which sound travels; breaking it causes a loud boom

questions.question: What did people see in the sky over Ohio and Pennsylvania?
What was the fireball likely to be?
Which agency confirmed the fireball fell near Cleveland?
Why did the fireball make a loud boom sound?
Was anyone hurt by the fireball?
What do scientists study meteors to learn about?

background_read: A meteor is a piece of rock or dust from space that burns up when it enters Earth's atmosphere, creating a bright streak called a fireball. Meteors are common, but seeing one during the day is rare. NASA and the National Weather Service help track these events to keep people safe and learn more about space. When a meteor travels very fast, it can break the sound barrier, making a loud noise like a boom. This event in Ohio and Pennsylvania was harmless and exciting for people who saw it.
Article_Structure: WHO: People in Ohio and Pennsylvania, NASA, and the National Weather Service
WHAT: A suspected meteor seen as a fireball and white streaks in the sky
WHERE: Over Ohio and Pennsylvania, near Cleveland
WHY: To report a natural space event that caused a loud boom but no harm

perspectives.description: Scientists see this as a chance to study meteors and learn about space.
People were excited and shared reports online about seeing the fireball.
